extra.message: User input from 'name' parameter is directly interpolated into SQL query using % operator. String formatting occurs before execution: VALUES ('%(name)s') % {'name': name}. This allows SQL injection attacks.

Jinja2 autoescape is explicitly disabled with autoescape=False. This allows user-controlled data rendered in templates to execute arbitrary JavaScript (XSS attacks). All template variables are rendered without HTML entity encoding.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The review() endpoint does not check if the user is authenticated or authorized. Any unauthenticated user can submit reviews via POST request. The function lacks @authorize() decorator present in logout().

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The evaluate() endpoint does not check if the user is authenticated or authorized. Any unauthenticated user can assign marks/grades to students. The function lacks @authorize() decorator and should check if user is an instructor/admin.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: MD5 is used for password hashing (md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()). MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for password hashing. Use bcrypt, scrypt, PBKDF2, or Argon2 instead.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: CSRF middleware is commented out with '# csrf_middleware'. Cross-Site Request Forgery protection is disabled, allowing attackers to perform unauthorized actions on behalf of authenticated users.

Session cookie created with httponly=False. This allows JavaScript to access session cookies via document.cookie, enabling session hijacking via XSS vulnerabilities. Set httponly=True to prevent JavaScript access.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag

extra.message: The students() endpoint with POST method allows any user (authenticated or not) to create new student records. There is no authorization check to verify if the user has permission to create students.
The courses() endpoint with POST method allows any user (authenticated or not) to create new course records. There is no authorization check to verify if the user has permission to create courses.
